options.content:
#### Colour key — what the panel thresholds mean

🟢 good  ·  🟠 warning  ·  🔴 critical

| Signal | 🟢 Green | 🟠 Orange | 🔴 Red |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Oracle healthy** (`valid`) | Soft status usable for solvency | — | `valid=0` (stale, deviation, sanity, or unreadable) |
| **Oracle stale / deviation flags** | Flag clear (0) | — | Soft-status diagnostic flag set (1) |
| **Freshness (time to stale)** | ≥ 5 min of runway | < 5 min left | Stale — older than max-stale (≤ 0) |
| **Oracle deviation (bps)** | < 80% of tolerance band | Near band | Soft deviation flag / above band |
| **Market / cap utilization** | < 90% | 90–95% (market 98%) | over cap — nearly full |
| **Exporter — last scrape age** | < 60 s | 60–120 s | > 120 s — exporter stalled |

**How to read this board**

1. **Health strip** — anything red right now?
2. **MarketIndexView table** — snapshot: final/primary/anchor prices + soft flags (`valid` / `stale` / `deviation`) + blend status timestamp.
3. **Oracles row** — trends (prices, deviation vs band, provider freshness) + **oracle config** (strategy, max-stale, sanity band). Soft flags live in MIV; Oracles does not repeat the flag timeline.
4. **Markets** — size / util / rates / accrual age.
5. **Spokes** — liq curve, LTV/threshold, pause/freeze, usage & caps.
6. **Exporter health** — scrape trust.

**ABI names:** primary = `safe_price_wad`, anchor = secondary/`aggregator_price_wad`. Soft status is authority; provider-probe freshness is early-warning only.

_"Now" for staleness is the **ledger close time**._

panels.title: Timestamps (provider probe vs soft-status blend)
panels.description: Probe = worst-leg provider feed ts. Blend = MarketIndexView.price_timestamp from soft status.
